title: calceolaria micans
intro: Calceolaria micans, also known as the spotted fairybell or fairy bell, is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Andean region, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Calceolaria micans include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Calceolaria micans are typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
